title: WBS as the agreed scope baseline
overview: Explains how a baselined WBS establishes agreement on what will be built.
detailed_summary: Once the project WBS is baselined, it establishes agreement on the scope of what will be built. It becomes the focal point for defining schedulable activities and supports objective scope decisions. This baseline reflects stakeholder alignment on all in-scope deliverables before significant effort is spent on design and build.
content: Baselining the WBS means stakeholders have agreed on the deliverables the project will produce. That agreement creates a stable scope reference for activity definition, planning justification, and change control. Because the WBS captures the "what" of the project, it serves as the baseline against which additions, removals, and scope creep can be evaluated objectively.
relationships: Baselined WBS provides formal scope agreement
search_queries: What does it mean to baseline the WBS?
How does the WBS become the scope baseline?
common_questions: When is the WBS considered agreed?
What is the value of baselining the WBS?
How does a baselined WBS support control?
can_answer: How the WBS baseline is used later
examples: A baselined WBS can be used to decide whether a proposed deliverable is scope creep or approved scope.
best_practices: Seek stakeholder agreement before treating the WBS as the planning baseline.
Use the baseline consistently for change evaluation.
warnings: Using an unagreed WBS as a planning baseline can destabilize schedule and budget assumptions.
limitations: A baseline still needs approved updates when scope changes are accepted.
troubleshooting: If teams disagree about whether work is in scope, compare the work to the baselined WBS deliverables.
decision_rules: Treat the WBS as the formal scope baseline only after stakeholder agreement has been established.
